key_insight: A machine-learning potential enabled rapid screening of graphene-supported nitrogen-coordinated dual-atom catalysts, revealing that intermediate 2N–4N coordination most often gives near-optimal hydrogen adsorption for HER.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical computational route to identify low-cost, potentially synthesizable non-noble electrocatalysts for sustainable hydrogen production without relying on exhaustive DFT searches.
method: Screened 23 transition metals across 20 nitrogen coordination motifs on graphene-supported TM2@Nx structures.
Used a machine-learning potential benchmarked against DFT to evaluate HER Gibbs free energy of hydrogen adsorption (ΔGH*).
Ranked standout candidates with metallic or narrow-gap electronic character as promising HER catalysts.
evidence_signal: The model achieved near-DFT performance with a mean absolute error of 80 meV for Gibbs binding free energies while cutting computational cost by orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Predicted top candidates still need experimental synthesis and stability validation, since favorable computed adsorption energies do not guarantee real operating performance.
paper_title: Accelerated Discovery of Nitrogen-Coordinated Dual-Atom Hydrogen Evolution Reaction Electrocatalysts via Machine Learning Potentials
